name: Acha Pudding
intro: A quick, filling and nutritious breakfast eaten mainly in the northern part of Nigeria. Acha is the Hausa name for fonio - the gluten-free super grain indigenous to West Africa. Just like couscous, fonio swells easily when cooked so you only need to start oﬀ with a small amount.
method: Bring the water and milk to a boil in a pot, then add the fonio, nutmeg and cinnamon. Cover and cook for 10-12 minutes on low heat, stirring often, until it starts to thicken. You should be left with a fluﬀy, creamy porridge. Sweeten with sugar or syrup of your choice.
Serve hot on its own or with a topping of roasted groundnuts and/or fresh fruit to make it more filling.
